title: Siya Goyal, Chetan Chaudhary now blame each other for plotting Ketan's murder
content: A shocking murder plot has unraveled in Pune after a real estate director's death at Lohagad Fort. His fiancée and her lover, initially accused of the crime, are now pointing fingers at each other. Investigators revealed a meticulously planned conspiracy involving multiple failed attempts before the fatal push, with the couple allegedly driven by an unwillingness to marry and a desire to avoid family disgrace.

title: 'I will kill you': Shiv Sena MP Sanjay Dina Patil, among 6 UBT defectors, abuses journalists
content: Shiv Sena MP Sanjay Dina Patil allegedly threatened journalists with dire consequences when questioned about his daughter's political stance and previous controversial remarks. Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde intervened, urging Patil to express regret for his outburst, suggesting it was a reaction to personal attacks. Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis stated he would gather more information before commenting on the incident.

title: FIFA World Cup: How the best eight third-placed teams will be determined — Explained
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026's group stage is nearing its end, with 13 teams already securing spots in the Round of 32. A thrilling race is underway for the eight best third-placed teams to also advance. FIFA's ranking system, prioritising points, goal difference, goals scored, conduct, and FIFA rankings, will determine these crucial qualifiers. Several high-stakes matches remain, promising to finalise the knockout picture by Sunday morning.

title: EPFO 3.0: ATM, UPI-linked withdrawals & more - what reforms mean for subscribers
content: EPFO 3.0 aims to deliver quicker automated claim settlements, instant withdrawals, multilingual self-service facilities and seamless payroll-integrated contributions.

title: Days after Meta CTO admitted ‘worst morale at company’, tech giant makes U-turn on 2 policies
content: Meta has reversed its controversial policy forcing engineers into an AI task force, allowing employees personal choice after widespread backlash. This move follows recent mass layoffs and an AI reorganisation that sparked unrest. The company also acknowledged past mistakes in its aggressive AI push, with CTO Andrew Bosworth admitting morale is at historic lows.  Concerns over an AI tracking tool have also led to its pause.

title: Elon Musk loses trillionaire status after Accenture-triggered market wipeout
content: Elon Musk lost his trillionaire status barely two weeks after earning it, as a tech sell-off sparked by Accenture's weak guidance wiped over $360 billion from his fortune. SpaceX shares crashed more than 30% from their June peak, while Tesla slid 5.8%, dropping Musk's net worth to $957 billion. With both stocks holding up nearly his entire wealth, the rout hit harder than any rival. A 6% SpaceX rebound could restore his trillionaire title.

title: From Spike to Hellfire: Inside India’s layered tank-killers
content: India's Army is bolstering its anti-tank capabilities with a mix of legacy and modern missile systems. From reliable Russian Konkurs and Franco-German MILAN to advanced Israeli Spike, the infantry is well-equipped. Main battle tanks and Apache helicopters also contribute with integrated guided missiles and aerial precision strikes, ensuring a robust defense against evolving armored threats across diverse terrains.

title: US holds Valiant Shield wargames in western Pacific; India deepens Vietnam push
content: Valiant Shield 2026, a major multinational military exercise, is underway across the Indo-Pacific, involving the US and allies like Australia, Japan, Canada, and New Zealand. This drill emphasizes advanced capabilities and interoperability in air, land, sea, space, and cyber domains. Meanwhile, the Indian Navy is bolstering maritime partnerships with a recent port call in Vietnam, reinforcing regional security cooperation and India's commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific.

title: West Bengal's Banglar Awaas Yojana: Eligibility, required documents and how to apply
content: The Banglar Awaas Yojana in West Bengal aims to provide a sturdy home to individuals lacking proper housing or living in unsafe conditions. Eligible participants can receive Rs 1.3 lakh distributed over several installments and also benefit from 95 days of unskilled labor wages. Applicants must not own a pucca house and must be listed on the Permanent Waiting List from 2011.

title: $1.5 billion-crypto connection: How North Korean heist ended in Iran's central bank
content: A massive crypto heist, initially linked to North Korea, has unraveled a complex financial web connecting to Iran's central bank. Investigators discovered stolen funds moving through wallets associated with Iran's central bank before reaching CoinEx, a key exchange facilitating Iranian crypto users. This revelation underscores the growing role of digital assets in Iran and the challenges in monitoring cross-border transactions.

title: NBA trade rumor: Lakers eye Cavaliers center to support Luka Doncic
content: The Los Angeles Lakers would reportedly pursue a sign-and-trade sending LeBron James to the Cleveland Cavaliers for center Jarrett Allen, ESPN's Brian Windhorst said June 24, 2026 on ESPN Cleveland radio. James, an unrestricted free agent carrying a roughly $59.5 million cap hold, made $52.6 million last season. The proposal has gained traction because Allen would address the Lakers' long-standing need at center while complementing Luka Doncic and newly re-signed Austin Reaves.

title: India vs Ireland T20Is timings changed ahead of Sooryavanshi's expected debut
content: Cricket Ireland has shifted the start times for both T20Is against India to 1:30 pm local time, an hour earlier than usual. This adjustment aims to avoid a direct clash with India's crucial Women's T20 World Cup match against Australia on June 28. Broadcasters have agreed to the revised schedule, ensuring fans can catch more of both exciting encounters.

title: Russia denies report of pressuring Belarus to deepen role in Ukraine war
content: Belarus served as a staging ground for Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022, allowing Russian forces to launch their offensive from its territory. The conflict has now entered its fifth year. Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy has repeatedly said in recent months that he believes Moscow is seeking to draw Belarus further into the war.

title: Megha Ghadge on Shraddha Kapoor's 'Eetha' teaser, says she performed Lavani while pregnant
content: Lavani artist Megha Ghadge has voiced profound respect for Shraddha Kapoor's portrayal of Vithabai Narayangaonkar, describing the teaser as both emotional and impactful. Ghadge, an award recipient in Vithabai's honor, shares a passion for performance and believes the film will introduce the iconic artist's legacy to a broader audience. She also notes the vital need to maintain Lavani's genuine essence as the genre evolves.

title: DDA releases data on vacant lands under its control in big transparency push
content: The Delhi Development Authority (DDA) has recently launched a new initiative to promote transparency by displaying information about vacant land parcels on its official website. This development follows Supreme Court rulings affirming land acquisition procedures. Citizens are encouraged to access this data for informational purposes only, as it is not intended for legal or commercial use. Regular updates to this information will be provided by DDA.

title: What Indian basketball learnt from Asia's best at the NBA Rising Stars Invitational
content: Japan. South Korea. China. Australia. Through the first three days of the NBA Rising Stars Invitational, the same basketball nations have continued to separate themselves from the rest. Watching India's lone representatives up close offered perhaps the clearest explanation why.

title: FIR against Army commanding officer, 40 soldiers for 'assaulting' cops at J&K police station
content: Jammu and Kashmir Police have registered a case against several Army personnel, including a commanding officer, for allegedly storming a police station in Kishtwar. The incident reportedly followed the seizure of a private vehicle owned by an Army official for traffic violations. The FIR details an assault on police personnel, damage to government property, and accusations of an attempt to cause fatal injuries.

title: Bangladesh, China agree to deepen Teesta cooperation during Rahman's Beijing visit
content: Rahman travelled to the Chinese city of Dalian from Kuala Lumpur on June 22 after attending the World Economic Forum in Malaysia, which marked his first overseas visit since assuming office earlier this year. He later travelled by high-speed train to Beijing on Wednesday and is scheduled to meet President Xi Jinping, Premier Li Qiang and other senior Chinese leaders.

title: Woman's cries for help, speeding SUV trigger late-night chase in UP
content: A dramatic late-night incident near Meerut's Brahmpuri Metro Station saw a speeding SUV with an open rear door and a woman's cries for help. A local trader pursued the vehicle for nearly 3 km, hearing screams and shouts of abduction. Despite eyewitness accounts and video evidence, police are investigating it as a possible domestic dispute, stating a woman, man, and child were in the car heading towards Delhi.

title: ‘Felt humiliated’: How row over train door turned deadly in Mumbai local; passenger stabbed
content: A shocking act of violence unfolded on a Mumbai local train as a clothing store employee allegedly stabbed a co-passenger to death following an argument over a train door. The dispute, reportedly sparked by rain splashing passengers, escalated when the accused, feeling humiliated, repeatedly attacked the victim. Authorities apprehended the suspect in Panvel while he was attempting to flee.

title: TS SSC supplementary result declared: Here's direct link to download marks memo
content: Good news for students! The Telangana Board of Secondary Education has released the TS SSC Supplementary Result 2026 today, June 25. Those who took the Class 10 supplementary exams can now check their scores online at bse. telangana. gov. in by entering their roll number. This comes after the main exam results were announced in April, with a high pass rate of 95.15%

title: Mumbai’s housing makeover: Three huge slum clusters up for redevelopment
content: Mumbai is set for a massive slum redevelopment drive, following the success of the Juhu Galli project. Three large slum pockets – Antop Hill, Majaswadi, and Behrampada – are slated for transformation. This initiative aims to provide dignified housing for citizens and create a slum-free city. Meanwhile, Delhi has also approved a new policy to redevelop its JJ clusters through public-private partnerships, extending eligibility to more families.

title: Missiles, drones, damaged bases and depleted stockpiles: Trump faces massive Iran war bill
content: US military actions against Iran have incurred significant costs, estimated between $34 billion and $42 billion by CSIS. Congress now faces the challenge of funding these unbudgeted expenses, with the Pentagon preferring supplemental appropriations to protect modernization programs. The conflict's financial toll includes substantial spending on munitions, equipment losses, and base damage, with broader economic impacts also noted.

title: Asked about Virat, Rohit and Dhoni's absence at wedding, star pacer's reply goes viral
content: Indian pacer Akash Deep tied the knot with Akshita Raj in Varanasi, sharing joyous moments on social media. When asked about the absence of cricket stars like Dhoni, Kohli, and Sharma, Akash humorously quipped about the potential chaos their presence might cause in Banaras. This personal milestone follows his recovery from a recent injury that sidelined him from international and IPL action.

title: PM Modi meets Amazon CEO Andy Jassy; company commits $48 bn investment in India
content: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced a significant $13 billion investment to boost AI and cloud infrastructure in India by 2030, bringing the total planned investment to over $21 billion. This expansion, alongside a commitment to enhancing its e-commerce network with new fulfillment centers and delivery stations, underscores Amazon's deep-rooted commitment to India's growth.

title: 'Cocktail 2' box office Day 7 [LIVE]: Shahid Kapoor's film eyes Rs 80 crore mark in India
content: Homi Adajania's 'Cocktail 2' featuring Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon, and Rashmika Mandanna is making waves at the box office, having surpassed Rs 100 crore globally and nearing Rs 70 crore in India alone. Although reviews have been mixed, the romantic drama capturing a contemporary love triangle had a robust opening weekend, and its impressive performance on the seventh day suggests it may reach even greater heights soon.

title: Neymar matches Pelé with one of Brazil's rarest World Cup feat
content: Neymar made his long-awaited World Cup return in Brazil’s 3-0 win over Scotland, becoming only the fourth Brazilian to appear at four World Cups and just the second after Pelé to wear Brazil’s iconic No. 10 shirt across four editions. The 34-year-old impressed in a brief cameo as Brazil finished top of Group C and advanced unbeaten to the Round of 32.

title: Ahead of UP elections, BJP announces new team: Rajnath Singh's son among 19 vice-presidents
content: The BJP has unveiled its new Uttar Pradesh unit office-bearers, signaling a strategic move ahead of the 2027 assembly elections. Key positions including Vice Presidents, General Secretaries, Secretaries, and Regional Presidents have been filled. This reshuffle aims to bolster the party's organizational strength and campaign readiness across the state, with a focus on key leadership roles and morcha presidents.

title: Ex-AAP candidate found dead in flat; family alleges murder by live-in partner
content: A 23-year-old woman's death in Rajkot has ignited accusations of murder from her family, who point to her live-in partner, Aslam Sama. Nandani Bosmiya was found hanging in her apartment, with relatives alleging frequent disputes and past assaults by Sama, who is reportedly married. The family questions the scene's circumstances, citing an unlatched main door and an open balcony. Police are investigating all angles, awaiting forensic postmortem results.

title: Strait of Hormuz reopens: Middle East oil exports to climb to 4-month high
content: The Strait of Hormuz is reopening, paving the way for Middle Eastern fuel oil exports to reach a four-month high in June. Iraq and Saudi Arabia are leading this surge, with Iraq notably diversifying routes through Syria. While shipments are increasing, they remain below pre-war levels, and cautious optimism prevails due to ongoing negotiation uncertainties. This development follows an interim US-Iran agreement.

title: CBSE class 10 board result: Pass percentage jumped from 29% to 48% in recent years
content: Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) is poised to release Class 10 Second Board Results 2026 soon, with lakhs of students eagerly awaiting their scores. This year marks a significant shift with the introduction of a new two-exam system, offering students a second chance to improve their performance. Previous supplementary exam data reveals a notable increase in pass percentages from 2024 to 2025, indicating evolving student outcomes.

title: ITR filing FY 2025-26: Old vs new income tax regime - how salaried taxpayers can lower tax outgo
content: ITR filing FY 2025-26: The two regimes differ fundamentally in structure. The new tax regime offers lower slab rates but largely eliminates exemptions and deductions. The old tax regime, in contrast, allows taxpayers to claim a range of benefits.

title: 33-year-old man found dead in actress Krishi Thapanda's apartment, probe underway
content: A 33-year-old man, Vaishak, was discovered deceased under suspicious circumstances at Kannada actress Krishi Thapanda's Bengaluru apartment. Police confirmed Vaishak knew the actress and had visited her residence on Wednesday night. Thapanda was reportedly out of town. Authorities are investigating the unnatural death, examining CCTV footage, and awaiting the post-mortem report to ascertain the cause.

title: Türkiye’s next-gen unmanned fighter demonstrates precision strike capability
content: Turkey's Bayraktar Kizilelma, a stealthy jet-powered unmanned fighter, has successfully completed firing tests, marking a significant advancement in its drone capabilities. This development builds on the global success of the Bayraktar TB2. Meanwhile, India is developing its own loyal wingman drone, the CATS Warrior, as part of its Combat Air Teaming System program, highlighting a global trend towards manned-unmanned teaming in aerial warfare.

title: Punjab Chief Minister Mann denies he is in controversial video
content: This blackmailer, who is holding this mask in his hand, put it on some other person and made a video, says

title: How a gate failure reshaped safety planning at Tungabhadra dam in Karnataka
content: What began as a sudden structural failure in one bay of the historic reservoir soon grew into a much wider concern over the health of the entire gate system at one of south India’s most important inter-State irrigation projects

title: India’s envoy to Bangladesh Dinesh Trivedi assumes office, announces resumption of travel visas
content: Visa applications can be submitted starting June 28, Mr. Trivedi said after presenting his credentials to Bangladesh President Mohammed Shahabuddin; Union govt. accords Mr. Trivedi the status of a Union Minister for ceremonial functions

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay opposes proposed disinvestment of Centre’s equity in NLC India Ltd.
content: “Public sector enterprises of this nature, particularly those established and expanded with the sustained support of a host State over decades, should continue to remain firmly under effective Government ownership and control,” CM Vijay said in a letter to PM Modi

title: Man locks leopard inside home after saving dog in Masinagudi
content: Officials said that the dog was chased into its own home by a leopard; hearing the commotion, the owner quickly escaped from the house and locked the leopard inside the building, trapping it

title: Consider Teesta water sharing deal under ‘current circumstances’: Bangladesh FM post BJP’s Bengal win
content: Mamata percieved as hurdle in Teesta talks; ahead of Beijing trip, Bangladesh FM says, “Of course, Teesta will be discussed with China”; Dhaka demands greater share of Teesta waters

title: No rush for textbooks or rare books, yet Hyderabad’s Koti book market has its regulars in digital age
content: People who want to keep away digital devices while preparing for competitive exams, those without easy internet access are among people who still visit the old bookstores in the subway.

title: Ananthan Kaadu movie review: A flat, old-fashioned political potboiler lacking novelty
content: The filmmaker and the screenwriter seem to have taken the period setting of the film in the early 1990s a little too seriously, for the writing and making have a visible hangover of that era

title: Scrap NEET, return medical admissions to States: PDSO protest in Visakhapatnam
content: The students’ body said NEET, JEE and CUET were putting aspirants under severe stress, and sought the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: NEET: Beyond paper leaks, the real vulnerabilities lie elsewhere | Explained
content: While extraordinary security measures may safeguard printed question papers, deeper risks arising from institutional blind spots, insider networks, and powerful commercial interests remain largely unaddressed

title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy meets PM Modi; announces plans to invest additional $13 billion in India on cloud, AI
content: The company plans to launch more than 20 new fulfillment centers and over 100 new last mile delivery stations this year, bringing faster and reliable deliveries to customers nationwide, especially in tier 3 and 4 cities.

title: Civil Aviation Ministry, DIAL launch Air Suvidha 2.0 portal for Ebola health screening at points of entry in India
content: The launch follows the World Health Organisation’s declaration of Ebola/Bundibugyo virus disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda as a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) last month

title: Withdraw ₹15,000-crore True-Up burden, refund consumers: Y.S. Sharmila
content: The State Congress chief said two rounds of True-Up charges had placed a ₹15,654-crore burden on consumers in two years, and called the government’s power report a collection of half-truths

title: Kerala High Court receives PIL on unsafe access road to Arookutty Community Health Centre
content: Petitioner states that damaged condition of road ‘severely hinders’ ambulance movement, especially in adverse weather, and affects health care access to the elderly, pregnant women, schoolchildren, and emergency service users

title: Tiruvallur ammonia gas leak: Woman from Odisha dies, taking toll to 11
content: Currently, 67 persons – 64 women and three men – are undergoing treatment. Of them, 16 patients are on ventilator support, 21 are receiving nasal oxygen, and 30 patients are stable

title: Godavari-Cauvery river linking must be taken up as national project, says Andhra Pradesh CM N. Chandrababu Naidu
content: The Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister said inter-linking of rivers is the only sustainable answer to the simultaneous challenges of flood and drought.

title: New Tungabhadra gates will help address decades-old distress in basin: Telangana CM A. Revanth Reddy
content: He said the gathering of leaders from Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana on a common platform reflected a collective resolve to address the problems of the Tungabhadra command area beyond political considerations

title: New gates of Tungabhadra dam inaugurated, three States vow to protect interests of farmers
content: Mr. Shivakumar said the three States discussed key concerns raised by farmers, including the proposed Navali balancing reservoir, and the need to remove accumulated silt from the reservoir to improve water conservation and storage

title: Who is Jagadish Palaniswamy? A look at Tamil Nadu CM Vijay’s close aide and Private Secretary
content: Palanisamy, Vijay’s close aide, film producer, and the head of a talent management company, was appointed as Private Secretary to the Chief Minister

title: Union Government to roll out major desilting plan for dams across India, says Jal Shakti Minister C.R. Patil
content: The Union Minister praised the coming together of Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana on a common platform, saying the participation of the three CMs in the Tungabhadra event reflected a spirit of cooperation over a shared water resource

title: White Paper on T.N. electricity department puts debt at ₹2.47 lakh crore: Minister Nirmalkumar
content: The Electricity Minister said the White Paper was presented to show that the tariff revision implemented by the previous government in 2022, which led to a spike in electricity charges for all categories of consumers, benefited neither the electricity department nor the consumers, as substantial expenditure was incurred on power purchase agreements

title: Anbumani claims 43% seats vacant in T.N. government arts and science colleges due to falling standards
content: In a statement, he said of the 1.26 lakh seats available in 181 government arts and science colleges, only around 71,500 seats had been filled so far, leaving over 55,000 vacant

title: How everyday Kerala is being reimagined in an award-nominated illustration series
content: lllustrator Muhammed Sajid’s portrait series is in contention at the AOI World Illustration Awards 2026, spotlighting fish sellers, flower vendors, postmen and other familiar faces of Kerala’s streets

title: Kolkata warehouse tragedy: Death toll climbs to nine, CM orders audit of building plans
content: The West Bengal Chief Minister said that an FIR has been registered and five persons have been arrested

title: Jaishankar calls for closer cooperation with South Korea at Jeju Forum
content: Mr. Jaishankar also called for “greater capacity and opportunities” for the Global South to create new factors of growth, while highlighting that “the interests of a few” nations are being “openly prioritised” - without naming any country

title: Kerala CM Satheesan shifts to official residence ‘Cliff House’
content: V.D. Satheesan had been staying at Cantonment House, the official residence of the Leader of the Opposition, while maintenance work was being carried out at Cliff House

title: In major organisational rejig, BJP announces new office bearers in Uttar Pradesh
content: According to the list issued by State BJP president Pankaj Chaudhary, the party has appointed 19 vice-presidents and eight general secretaries. Nineteen State Ministers have also been appointed as office bearers

title: If a passport is not proof of citizenship, then what is? | Explained
content: The MEA in a statement said that a passport is a travel document, not a citizenship document. What does the law actually say?

title: Symbolism or substance? Tamil Nadu’s public health professionals question gold ring scheme
content: The gold ring may be intended as a celebration of birth, but for many within the public health sector, the more pressing need is investment in the workforce and infrastructure that makes safe deliveries possible, say health officials and doctors

title: VCK wants CM Vijay to reconsider gold ring scheme
content: Vijay’s remarks in the Assembly, in which he appeared to mock the electoral loss of former CM Stalin in Kolathur, were not “befitting the dignity associated with the office of the Chief Minister”, says Thirumavalavan

title: Jawahirullah urges TVK government to reconsider gold ring scheme
content: Questioning the government's priorities, he said spending crores of rupees on gold rings for newborns raises concerns when critical healthcare needs remain unmet

title: UDF stand on liquor tax cut may force Kerala govt to rethink move, says Congress leader Rajmohan Unnithan
content: Unnithan says liquor tax cut cannot not be implemented without discussion within UDF and without its approval

title: SIR exercise begins in Telangana; BLOs to visit houses from June 25 to July 24
content: People can search for their name in voters list through Chief Electoral Officer (CEO) Telangana website (https://ceotelangana.nic.in/)

title: Why has India-U.S. trade deal been delayed? | Explained
content: From India’s Russian oil purchases to legal battles in Washington, the road to India-U.S. trade deal has become increasingly uncertain, and The Hindu explains why

title: India initiates anti-dumping probe against three Chinese products
content: Four applications have been filed by separate domestic firms before Commerce Ministry's Directorate General of Trade Remedies to initiate the probe

title: NIA arrests local political leader over SIR-related violence in West Bengal
content: Sayem Chowdhary alias Babu Chowdhary, a political leader from Mothbari (Malda), was taken into custody by an NIA team after he was questioned at the agency's branch office in Kolkata, an NIA statement said.

title: BJP backs NCERT's move to include ‘Emergency’ chapter in Class 9 textbook
content: The newly developed social science textbook describes Emergency as "one of the major challenges" to democracy in India and one in which a majority of fundamental rights were suspended

title: Tungabhadra river: Origin, course, dam project and inter-State water sharing explained
content: The Tungabhadra reservoir remains a vital inter-State lifeline, serving agricultural command areas across Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana

title: Kerala HC directs MoRTH to finalise approval for Vizhinjam-Navaikulam Outer Ring Road project
content: A petition was filed by Vizhinjam ORR Land Owners Welfare Movement, alleging that no compensation had been paid to the owners of the land acquired for the project

title: Former IAS officer Sujata Rout Karthikeyan joins Biju Janata Dal
content: Former Odisha CM Naveen Patnaik asserts that he would lead the party in next elections, putting to rest the succession debate in the regional party

title: TVK banner obscures police outpost signboard in Coimbatore, taken down after criticism
content: The issue drew criticism on social media and among members of the public, following which the banner was removed on Thursday

title: Raut seeks action against rebel MP Sanjay Patil over 'threats'; Fadnavis says police will act
content: Responding to the spat between the two leaders, Devendra Fadnavis said no one should be intimidated by threats and asserted that the state would not tolerate any such acts

title: Chennai’s Dr Priya Selvaraj on surviving a crevasse fall and summiting Mount Everest
content: Dr Priya Selvaraj trained for four months to summit Mount Everest. The daunting climb there and back, scaling the highest mountain in the world at 8,848.86 metres, taught her many lessons and even had her fighting for her life. Back in Chennai, this granddaughter of legendary actor Gemini Ganesan, recounts the gritty details of her journey

title: Former director of medical education in Karnataka Dr. N. Rajeeva Shetty passes away in Bengaluru
content: Dr. Rajeeva Shetty made significant contributions to dental education, research, and healthcare administration

title: I prefer solutions over disputes, says Telangana CM on river water sharing issues with Karnataka and AP
content: Central Govt decides to constitute a high level committee to resolve water sharing problems between the three southern States — Telangana, Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka

title: Money laundering case: Actor Jacqueline Fernandez withdraws plea from Supreme Court
content: Jacqueline Fernandez, who was was summoned by the ED several times in connection with the probe, was named an accused for the first time in a supplementary charge sheet filed by the agency

title: UDF government’s stance on PM SHRI unconstitutional
content: The MoU was entered into by the Secretary, Education Department, Government of Kerala, and the Additional Secretary, Department of Education, Government of India, and not in the name of the President and the Governor as mandated under Article 299. Hence, the said MoU is a nullity

title: Explained | Why NMC is phasing out PG diploma medical courses from 2027
content: The National Medical Commission has directed medical colleges offering PG diploma courses to apply for conversion of those seats into MD/MS broad-specialty degree seats; this move is the culmination of a policy shift that has been under way for nearly two decades

title: Thiruvananthapuram Corporation witnesses high drama as LDF steps up protest; Mayor injured
content: Minor scuffle breaks out between LDF and BJP councillors as LDF councillors block Mayor V.V. Rajesh from entering the office. LDF councillors have demanded removal of BJP councillor R. Sugathan arrested under KAAPA

title: Politically-patronised private investment crowding out broad-based private investment: Congress
content: Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh also alleged that they are now witnessing "politically-patronised" private investment itself crowding out broad-based private investment

title: CMRL pay-off case: Ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi’s daughter Veena appears before ED again
content: Questioning is after Enforcement Directorate on Wednesday obtained copies of 134 documents collected by Serious Fraud Investigation Office in connection with the case

title: Maharashtra Assembly: Opposition raises Mumbai local train murder; Speaker asks govt. to make statement
content: Congress MLA Nana Patole termed the incident “extremely serious” and said it concerned the safety of ordinary citizens travelling on Mumbai’s suburban rail network that ferries nearly 80 lakh commuters every day

title: Lionel Messi’s 39th birthday celebrations in Bengal’s Ichhapur
content: A priest, a life sized statue, a 39-pound cake and a tea seller’s lifelong devotion. Inside Lionel Messi’s annual birthday celebrations in West Bengal’s Ichhapur

title: Video: Siya Goyal's Cafe Rendezvous With Lover Before Pushing Fiance To Death
content: According to police, Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary met at a cafe in Pune on June 18 to plot the murder. During that meeting, they discussed how to carry out the killing and identified specific locations on the fort from which Ketan Agarwal could be pushed.

title: Raghav Chadha Demands Bhagwant Mann's Resignation Over Video Row
content: The Haryana Police registered a case on Tuesday on a complaint from a man who alleged that he was approached to procure the fabricated forensic report of the video.

title: "Jews Sick Of You": Book Reveals Trump's Outburst At Netanyahu In 2025 Call
content: The book, "Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump", talks about Trump's second term as president, during which the Republican leader brokered the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.

title: Spain Records 212 Deaths Due To Heatwave In 4 Days: Report
content: The number of heat-related deaths in Spain between May 16 and September 30 last year hit 3,832, an 87.6-per cent increase from the same period in 2024.

title: "Result Of Sins": Suvendu Adhikari Slams Trinamool Over Kolkata Roof Collapse
content: Addressing the Assembly on Thursday, Suvendu Adhikari said the sanctioned building plan carried the signature of former Kolkata mayor and senior Trinamool leader Firhad Hakim.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Britain Seems To Have A New National Hobby: Firing Its Prime Ministers
content: Britain's Prime Ministers are dropping like mayflies, and nobody seems to be able to stop the mayhem.

title: Death Count In Kolkata Warehouse Collapse Rises To 11
content: Five more people were rescued from the debris in the morning, taking the total number of victims pulled out from under the rubble to 30, according to sources involved in the operation.

title: 40 Soldiers Face FIR For Storming J&K Police Station After Vehicle Seizure
content: In the FIR, police have alleged that the attack was pre-planned and several policemen have been injured.

title: "Grateful": Venezuela's Delcy Rodriguez Thanks PM Modi For Support
content: The dual earthquakes, which included a massive 7.5-magnitude tremor that struck just 40 seconds after a 7.2-magnitude foreshock, have claimed over 160 lives and left hundreds of others injured.

title: Bangladeshi Man Without Valid Documents Arrested In Kerala
content: The accused, identified as Mohammad Rathon Islam, was arrested from a plywood company at Vandamattam in Ernakulam's Kuruppampady area.

title: How To Live A Long Life? Scientists Tap Brazilian Sisters Aged 103, 104, 109
content: Findings from the three sisters' case could help scientists better understand why some people remain physically and cognitively resilient at exceptionally advanced ages.

title: Businessman Found Dead At Actor Krishi Thapanda's Bengaluru Home
content: According to the police, the incident occurred around 8:30 pm last night, while Krishi Thapanda was away in Yelahanka.

title: 164 Dead, 971 Injured After Powerful Earthquakes Hit Venezuela
content: The quakes were among the strongest to strike Venezuela in more than a century and could be felt throughout the region.

title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: At Least 164 Dead, 971 Injured, Says Acting President
